transcript.text: This meeting is being recorded.
My college football team won a big game on Saturday, so that was a great way to head into the next work week.
Good, you're high on life.
I love it.
I love it.
Exactly, exactly.
Well, it's a pleasure to meet you.
Just as a quick intro, I'm the founding AE here at GrowthX, so I'm going to do my best to give you as much context on who we are, how we partner, and what to expect from GrowthX in this call today.
Wonderful.
I'd love to hear about it.
Awesome, awesome.
I guess just to kind of start off in terms of context from your side, know Allison introduced you.
How much do you know about what we do here?
Very high level only.
She described it to the best of her ability, but it didn't go into great detail.
So, you know, you can kind of assume a fresh start.
Okay, perfect.
Perfect.

I think at a high level, just to give you like some initial context, you could see GrowthX as an end-to-end partner, like an agency partner with everything from SEO and GEO strategy to content creation to daily publishing in your CMS.
A lot of what we focus on is really helping clients drive organic traffic and, you know, doing that through like editorial content creation.
I guess, you know, where would you say like the company's at in their content journey, given that you guys are a little more early stage than some of the clients that we typically work with?
I've been doing that myself, which is, it's a lot of work and I can use some help.
I have, you know, at this moment, I have a blog on the website that we publish too.
I'm a contributing author to a number of different publications, so I, you know, I get content out that way.
Uh.
I do some podcasts, you know, but all in all, we haven't seen a lot of organic graphic yet, you know, like it's, I have somebody helping with a website on SEO, you know, we're always updating that.
There's a million things that I hear we should do to improve SEO, which we do, and, you know, the difference that it makes is fairly small, and so I'm learning as I go.
But, you know, it's a, it kind of takes a village, I think.
It's a lot of, there's a lot to do.

Absolutely, and I guess when you think about, like, your priorities in general, like, do you see, you know, creating, like, a broader blog and, you know, building out more content as high on your priorities, or, you know, are you focused on some other things right now?
just kind of want to get a sense of, you know, like, when we might be the right partner for you.
There's, there's two...
In my mind, two sides to the equation.
There's driving organic traffic and building pipeline that way.
And I see that as sort of a long game, right?
Yep.
It's slowly build momentum there, but eventually have, you know, regular stream of new customers coming and signing up on our website.

And then the other half is, you know, I'm the process of looking at how to bring in some BDRs and get them on the phone and start doing outreach to our customer base to build pipeline that way, which I see more as a short-term game.
And we're both, we're getting started, really just getting started on both of those fronts.
We've primarily over the last year been focused on product, getting the product developed, raising money, know, all the things.
Just getting foundations in place.
But we're at a point now where we've got 10 or so production customers, and we're hardening the product based on their usage and their feedback.
And I would say in the next 30 days, we'd like to start selling in earnest.
That's awesome.
That's some great traction, and you guys are moving quick.
Love to see it.
How about I go through a quick presentation on GrowthX?
I'll go through some, you know, customer examples, and then I'll show you like the workflow, the AI workflows that we leverage to actually create the content.
Would that give you kind of that full context?
Yeah, that'd be great.
That'd be great.
Perfect.
Let's do it.
Just share my screen here.
Awesome.
So, you know, a lot of the reason why we're seeing so much demand at GrowthX, our services are so impactful, is the broader change in content marketing that you've been seeing.
Yeah, I and I'll Thank
Really the way that like every website in the world is getting disrupted by AI search, even like B2B software companies are getting disrupted by AI on a daily, weekly basis.
And so what we're seeing, right, is clicks are disappearing.
Your potential audience is spending a lot of that first sales conversation with ChadGBT or something similar.
And so as a result, when people do hit your website and they come from AI search, they're showing a lot more intent.
SEMrush did a study about how they're 4.4 times more likely to convert.
They spend three times as long.
And so with GrowthX, our approach that's very audience-centric, it benefits both sides of search marketing, right?
The traditional SEO side where someone's just going to go in and maybe have like a simple query and you want to rank high.
Monograph is one of our customers here.
Or the AI search where the long tail is longer than it's ever been, every single person can go in and tell their...
Entire narrative.
And if you're not in the answer, it's almost like you don't exist.
And so what we do here is as we create content, we start audience first.
We go really deep into your business, your product, your ideal customer.
And then we leverage experts in the loop with our AI workflows to create content that satisfies the most important characteristics for GEO.
So we do deep research.
We make sure all the content can be attributed to objectives like third-party sources.
We'll make sure that it's comprehensive, up-to-date.
We'll even do refreshes on like a three- to six-month basis on the content.
And that's what's allowing us to see super quick results, right?
Like our customers, they don't want to hire big teams.

They don't want to try to buy tools like AirOps and then spend, you know, months trying to assemble the right workflows only to have that go out of date when a new model comes into the picture or, you know.
Maybe their costs scale out of control with all the tokens and API calls they're using.
And they're looking for an agency partner that can do much more than strategy or much more than content creation.
And so that's the high level around who we are, what we do, why it's different.
Any questions or anything you want me to double click on?
No, not so far.
That sounds good.
Perfect.
And then do you know much about Marcel's background?
Because I would say that he's like a huge reason like we are a different or a differentiated like agency than your average one.
So I could give you a quick summary of kind of his expertise.
I don't know much about Marcel, so I'd love to hear it.
Yeah, you bet.
So Marcel, he's always believed that brands should act more like publishers.

He likes to talk about how when he was working at IBM, you know, back in like 2011, 2012, they were in a position where they could just buy every publication with the if they wanted to with the size of their ad budget.
And so what he did is he built organic content engines at every stop.
In his career.
So at IBM, he built securityintelligence.com, which was their highest ROI marketing channel.
It drove like 17 million in pipeline in the first year.
It lived separately from ibm.com for over 10 years before they finally brought it back into the parent site.
And then at every company after that, you know, he did a version of the same thing.
So he was a marketing leader at HashiCorp from 6 million to 100 million.
He was the first and last CMO at scale through the acquisition by Meta.
And then at Deepgram, he did what he, you know, he did what brought him success at all these other stops.
But this time he built AI workflows instead of building out that big team of like editors, right, managing directors, people who could actually create the content at scale.
And so that's really the approach we have here at GrowthX is, you know, we're really services as software.
Start with this outcome of you want to drive higher intent organic traffic, leverage an internal platform that we're bringing our customers.
This is where all of our workflows are housed.
And then we have experts in the loop.
So our chief content officer, he was the editor-in-chief at TechCrunch for 10 years.
And then we've also got folks who ran SEO for Fanatics and TripAdvisor.
We've got a managing editor who built the G2.com website.
Every customer we work with gets a managing director, a managing editor, and a content manager as part of the engagement.
And then we really just focus on making sure the engagement is split into two pieces.
The first phase is the strategy sprint, where we're going to develop the artifacts that provide the context necessary to make the workflows effective.
We'll also do publishing during this first phase.
So you really understand what you'd be getting out of phase two, which is daily publishing.

And that's where we're really just putting the foot on the gas, right, and trying to create content at scale across all the opportunities, all the bets we want to make, all the opportunities we've aligned with you around.
And then you've got the ability to opt out, you know.
By the last week of phase one, if you didn't want to move into phase two.
So that's a little bit about how we work and, you know, what's happening under the hood with our team.
Okay.
In terms of content we've created for some of our customers, so Town is an example of, like, an earlier stage company.
They're seed stage and they're in the accounting space.
So for them, like, accuracy is everything.
You can't get tax information wrong, right?
And so, you know, people like to talk a lot about, like, AI hallucinating or how can we validate the accuracy of the content.
I'll show you our fact checker agent and how that works.
But Town is, like, an example of a customer who we ran, who we've created everything in their blog.
And, you know, you can see that, like, anything about the content we're creating for them, we're bringing in, like, quotes and, or, sorry, quotes from their team, customer quotes.
what, what,
But we're also, like, getting very technical and very specific around, you know, the content itself.
Got it.
Got it.
So your team is publishing to their blog, is that?
That's correct.
Cool.
Okay.
Makes sense.
Sorry, go ahead.
Keep going.
Yeah, no, I'll keep going here.
So editorial, like, that is our sweet spot for sure.
But it doesn't necessarily mean we're just going to do blogs.
For Augment Code, we've created, like, 90 guides over the last three months for them.
And these guides have, like, 2x the traffic of their blog in that time.
And they also had 300 signups last week that came from the guides that we've created.
So these guides, they're, like, thought leadership.
They're comparing, you know, tools in the AI coding space.
You know, and everything that you're seeing here from, like, the image generation to the content itself is...
It's content that we worked on for Augment.
You'll see that with Augment, right?
We had like a fast ROI.
So, you know, they partnered with us in April.
They've seen their organic traffic 10x in almost a six-month period.
They're starting to see our content show up in some of the main searches, right?
So like enterprise AI coding tools, there's two articles that we wrote for them right in the citations.
You'll also see that Augment's starting to come up in the answer.
And if we get like a bit more specific, you know, then they're starting to get quoted in the answer.
And so that's really our main goal is to make sure that we have a, you know, a very wide surface area for the topics you want to be the expert in.
And then once we've gotten that foundation in place, you know, then we can think about programmatic plays.
Or if you have like a programmatic play up front that you want us to tackle, we could also have that conversation.
So like Ramp, we created these.

Per Diem calculator pages, and for every city in America, you know, there's a page that we created for Ramp that allows someone to go in and calculate what's the Per Diem in Houston versus San Francisco, or, you know, we did their vendor database as well.
Can you say a little bit more about programmatic, please?
Help me understand what you mean by that.

Yeah, programmatic just meaning if there's an opportunity for us to create content at scale where each page is going to be slightly different, but we know what the inputs are up front, then we can do that for you to create, you know, a large breadth of content quickly as opposed to how every editorial piece, every blog post, it's going to be different, right?
So it's just like a PSEO use case where we're creating content at scale and helping you, you know, show up for every iteration, right?
of how someone might search.
What's the per diem in Houston versus San Francisco for this Ramp use case?
But also we did their expense classifier.
So, you know, what's the expense category for like every type of expense?
So that now like Ramp has content that shows up for every version of how someone might search.
Got it.
Got it.

The categories, for example.

And for...

Glossaries, we've seen those before.

So definitely, like, you know, we're down to get custom and down to think outside the box.
We just typically, like, focus on editorial.
oil is that first piece since it's so important to driving that initial traffic.

So the first step in that strategy sprint is to develop these really rich artifacts around your company and around your audience personas.
And so Engine, they're in the business travel space, and they had target verticals they were going after.
And so what we did was we created artifacts for each of those verticals.
And these artifacts, know, this is what's giving the context engineering to our workflows and also helping our team, right, become experts on your business.

Like, who are they?
What motivates them?
How do you win or lose their trust?
What's going to get them promoted, fired?
What's their pain points and frustrations?
And so once we've got these artifacts ready to go, well, that's when we can start creating.
creating content against the keyword opportunities that you see, against the topics that you want to have authority around.
And so moving into just an example of like a piece of content that we created, Biologica, they're in the supplement space in healthcare.
And so this is an example of an article that we're generating where we start with a keyword, a direction, and then the artifacts that I went over.
And then each step here is informing, you the creation of the publish ready piece.
So we'll create a brief as a first step.
We'll do deep research against that brief.
We'll put together an outline, a draft, and all the while our team has the ability to make edits directly in our platform with that context that I was talking about.
So they can, right, like make changes directly to the content, or they could even just give instructions, right?
Like add bullets to the intro section.
But what's cool about what I'm showing you here is that with each step, there's tons of things happening in the background.
So for this brief step, we're actually going in and we're fetching keyword traffic.
We're looking at the organic results, analyzing SERP factors, competitor content, and then all of that informs the brief that you're seeing in step one.
But if we took the research step, you know, what we're doing here is we're generating research questions.
We're performing deep research against each question and then validating it against credible, you know, third-party sources.

And then when you look at the fact checker, we'll take the entire article, we'll extract passages, and then we'll do that same research against, with different research questions on every single passage, validating it against a confidence interval and suggesting a rewrite if we can't prove the accuracy or validate like that what we're saying is
It's true.
And so it's a lot of this, you know, that goes into the systematic way that we're helping customers with GEO.
And we're always improving our platform and our workflows.
And so, you know, just for that research example that I was showing you, I could show you an example for Augment Code here where we've got this agentic workflow now.

And for that agentic workflow, we're actually going in and doing the research against a research plan where if we don't hit a certain quality score, we'll actually go in and run the research again until we hit a certain benchmark.
And so there's a lot that we're doing to just, you know, continue to make this systematic, quick time to value.
And then we're also doing as much as we can to bring any, like, and
Any resources that you have that you want to be leveraged in the content creation directly into the platform.
So we've got a knowledge base.
And with all the knowledge base, we'll actually make retrieval easier by having it exist like in this knowledge graph.
So there's a lot to growthx, obviously, but I just wanted to give you, you know, as much coverage as I could here, given how busy your schedule is.
Yeah, yep.
trying to point correctly, your team is doing this work?
Exactly.

How do I work with those folks?
What's typical?

Thanks.

back.

typical?
